symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable vomiting for 24 hours (unable to retain oral fluids)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ongoing enteral loss with failed oral replacement is the mechanism of the volume deficit.
Vomiting is non-specific; concurrent diarrhoea argues against obstruction.
Watery diarrhoea with nausea/vomiting is the typical viral gastroenteritis pattern and a major route of fluid loss.
Active diarrhoea and hyperactive bowel sounds are inconsistent with mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oliguria / anuria — no urine for 12 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anuria in the setting of volume loss indicates critical renal hypoperfusion.
Reduced urine output is a sensitive marker of falling circulating volume.

symptoms.symptom_name: New confusion / altered mental state (hypoactive delirium)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New delirium signals cerebral hypoperfusion/decompensation and end-organ effect of shock.
Delirium is also a feature of sepsis in older adults — keeps sepsis on the differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural dizziness / near-syncope on sitting, now bedbound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic symptoms reflect severe intravascular volume depletion; high falls/syncope risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extreme thirst and dry mouth/lips (patient/caregiver reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thirst and dry mucosae are consistent with significant fluid deficit.
Objective hypotension confirms decompensated shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from postural symptoms, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 115 with weak thready pulse (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia with a thready pulse is a hallmark of hypovolaemia — sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (>3 seconds) and dry mucous membranes on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective perfusion and hydration signs — require a clinician's hands; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with hyperactive bowel sounds and no guarding/rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign soft abdomen with hyperactive sounds effectively excludes obstruction — clinician examination, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (to confirm and grade AKI)

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised urea and creatinine with a raised urea:creatinine ratio consistent with pre-renal AKI; possible electrolyte derangement from GI losses
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: AKI severity and electrolyte disturbance go unquantified, risking unsafe fluid/electrolyte management

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods confirm and grade AKI — never available in real time via telehealth; requires in-person pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe localised abdominal pain and no haematemesis (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal severe pain/guarding and bilious/bloody vomiting lowers the probability of a surgical abdomen.
No haematemesis reduces the likelihood of a GI bleed as the cause of the collapse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile-to-low-grade temperature (37.6 C), no respiratory or urinary localising symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No high fever or localising source lowers, but in a frail elder does not exclude, occult sepsis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Decompensated hypovolaemic shock in a frail elder
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe hypovolaemic shock secondary to gastroenteritis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Anuria, new confusion, postural collapse, hypotension and tachycardia together define an emergency requiring urgent parenteral rehydration — not a routine infusion visit.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades pre-renal AKI and identifies electrolyte derangement; must be obtained on urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous blood gas / lactate and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses perfusion (lactate), haemoconcentration and possible infection in a shocked frail elder.
Screens for arrhythmia/ischaemia contributing to hypotension and for electrolyte effects; requires in-person equipment.
Cued only by the misleading encounter title; there is no evidence of iron deficiency and it is irrelevant to this acute emergency.
